TEXT.citation: Sung, L. M., Lily, I., Hsieh, F., & Lin, Z. (2008). Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics, 6(2.).

S.FORM: 'o vcongʉsi to ha hanako ci ihe ne spukunu ne noana'o au.
S.PHON: ʔo vʦoŋɨsi to hs hsnsko ʦi ihe ne spukunu ne nosnsʔo su.
S.FORM: 'o vcongʉsi to ha hanako ci ihe ne spukunu ne noana'o au.
S.PHON: ʔo vʦoŋɨsi to hs hsnsko ʦi ihe ne spukunu ne nosnsʔo su.
S.TRANSL: 以前hanako的先生在Spukunu那裡.
In the past, Hanako's husband was in Spukunu.

S.FORM: 'a 'a moso la no maezo fiho uh ne'e moso la ehtothomʉ.
S.PHON: ʔs ʔs moso ɗs no msezo fiho uh neʔe moso ɗs ehtothomɨ.

S.PHON: ʔs ʔs moso ɗs no msezo fiho uh neʔe moso ɗs ehtothomɨ.

TRANSL.notes: 以前戰爭的時候,那邊的人也有跟著去(打仗)

S.TRANSL: 以前戰爭的時候,那邊的人也有跟著去

TRANSL.notes: In the war, people there also followed (to fight).

S.TRANSL: In the war, people there also followed .

S.FORM: eh uh ho la uh ne'e lasi acnia engha X.
S.PHON: eh uh ho ɗs uh neʔe ɗssi sʦnis eŋhs *.
S.FORM: eh uh ho la uh ne'e lasi acnia engha X.

TRANSL.notes: 我每次去那邊他每次必定都講(這件事).

S.TRANSL: 我每次去那邊他每次必定都講 .

TRANSL.notes: Every time I went there, he definitely told (such a story).

S.TRANSL: Every time I went there, he definitely told .

S.FORM: zou mai ho la 'a 'so no eainca ntoho la mici XXXX m'ene ne maaya no na ino peisu.
S.PHON: zou msi ho ɗs ʔs ʔso no esinʦs ntoho ɗs miʦi **** mʔene ne mssjs no ns ino peisu.

S.PHON: zou msi ho ɗs ʔs ʔso no esinʦs ntoho ɗs miʦi **** mʔene ne mssjs no ns ino peisu.
S.TRANSL: 比方說, 他們曾經曾經想要向日本要錢.
For example, they had been considering to ask for money from Japan.

S.FORM: ia zou honci eainca 'a la e eh si av'a no.
S.PHON: is zou honʦi esinʦs ʔs ɗs e eh si svʔs no.
S.FORM: ia zou honci eainca 'a la e eh si av'a no.
S.PHON: is zou honʦi esinʦs ʔs ɗs e eh si svʔs no.
S.TRANSL: 對. 如果說... 該怎麼說....?
Yes. If.... How should we say...?

S.FORM: o'a ihe no fii.
S.PHON: oʔs ihe no fii.
S.FORM: o'a ihe no fii.
S.PHON: oʔs ihe no fii.

TRANSL.notes: 他們沒有給(錢)嗎?

S.TRANSL: 他們沒有給 嗎?

TRANSL.notes: They didn't give (them money)?

S.TRANSL: They didn't give ?

S.FORM: eh bochio ihe nana fii ho.
S.PHON: eh ɓoʦhio ihe nsns fii ho.
S.FORM: eh bochio ihe nana fii ho.
S.PHON: eh ɓoʦhio ihe nsns fii ho.
S.TRANSL: 據說他們給了.
It is heard that they gave some.

S.FORM: 'a ohe acʉha 'a ohe acʉha nenusi no mo yu yungefi ho oh ia mo n'a ako la unto.
S.PHON: ʔs ohe sʦɨhs ʔs ohe sʦɨhs nenusi no mo ju juŋefi ho oh is mo nʔs sko ɗs unto.
S.FORM: 'a ohe acʉha 'a ohe acʉha nenusi no mo yu yungefi ho oh ia mo n'a ako la unto.
S.PHON: ʔs ohe sʦɨhs ʔs ohe sʦɨhs nenusi no mo ju juŋefi ho oh is mo nʔs sko ɗs unto.
S.TRANSL: 不曉得他們到底是不是嫌不夠,他們還在不斷的運動當中.
Perhaps they complained the money insufficient, so they still keep on exercising.

S.FORM: ahoi ne auyusi ne an ancu ho la moso la ahoi to moso eh maezo yuyafo ho unto no enca te m'eovei.
S.PHON: shoi ne sujusi ne sn snʦu ho ɗs moso ɗs shoi to moso eh msezo jujsfo ho unto no enʦs te mʔeovei.

TRANSL.notes: 最初, 還沒有說XXX,...曾經 剛開始的時候, (那邊的人)也出來參加討錢的活動.

S.TRANSL: 最初, 還沒有說XXX,...曾經 剛開始的時候, 也出來參加討錢的活動.

TRANSL.notes: In the beginning, when XXXX had not been proposed, (people there) also stood out for the activity of asking for (the compensation money).

S.TRANSL: In the beginning, when XXXX had not been proposed, also stood out for the activity of asking for .

S.FORM: oh lasi acnia engha enivo.

S.FORM: Yingkaidoyola.

S.FORM: i'o i'o m i'o moso mcoi ua ohke cu fii.
S.PHON: iʔo iʔo m iʔo moso mʦoi us ohke ʦu fii.

S.PHON: iʔo iʔo m iʔo moso mʦoi us ohke ʦu fii.
S.TRANSL: 那時候死的是有給啦.

TRANSL.notes: For those dead, they gave (the money) indeed.

S.TRANSL: For those dead, they gave indeed.

S.FORM: mo c'o 'so at'ingi kakutia.
S.PHON: mo ʦʔo ʔso stʔiŋi kskutis.

S.PHON: mo ʦʔo ʔso stʔiŋi kskutis.

S.FORM: 'o a'ʉmtʉ 'o a'ʉmtʉ alʉ ak'i eh wodechinwaikong eh 'a ohe cu acʉha.
S.PHON: ʔo sʔɨmtɨ ʔo sʔɨmtɨ sɗɨ skʔi eh *o*eʦhin*sikoŋ eh ʔs ohe ʦu sʦɨhs.

S.TRANSL: 我的爺爺,他們已經全部.
My real grandfathers, they are all....

S.FORM: oh moso maeo amo'u si no hafa uh to inoconi ho ito ba'i.

S.TRANSL: My father also brought to my aunt and grandmother.

S.TRANSL: 有沒有多一點?

TRANSL.notes: Is (the money) much?

S.TRANSL: Is much?

S.FORM: na a'o.
S.PHON: ns sʔo.
S.FORM: na a'o.
S.PHON: ns sʔo.

Me?

S.FORM: zou.
S.PHON: zou.
S.FORM: zou.
S.PHON: zou.

Yes.

S.FORM: eh mo'u maeo to hookiyu.

S.FORM: eh mo'u maeo to hookiyu.
S.PHON: eh moʔu mseo to hookiju.
S.TRANSL: 我是領薪水的.

S.FORM: hookiyu eh zou na nia na nia hookiyu ho moso la heitai.

S.TRANSL: Salary, yes, salary when he was a solider.

S.FORM: zou oh 'a micu na'no man'i totuiha c'o nanamangosenyen.

S.TRANSL: 那沒有很多啦,三年才七萬五千元.
Not so much, there was only 75,000 Japanese Yens for 3 years.

S.FORM: mosu s'a mosu totuiha ho mosu.
S.PHON: mosu sʔs mosu totuihs ho mosu.
S.FORM: mosu s'a mosu totuiha ho mosu.
S.PHON: mosu sʔs mosu totuihs ho mosu.
S.TRANSL: 你是...你是三年在...
For three years, you were...

S.FORM: mi'o yone'e.
S.PHON: miʔo joneʔe.
S.FORM: mi'o yone'e.
S.PHON: miʔo joneʔe.
S.TRANSL: 我在那裡.
I was there.

S.FORM: te n'a peela eainca ak'a ao'pova.
S.PHON: te nʔs peeɗs esinʦs skʔs soʔpovs.
S.FORM: te n'a peela eainca ak'a ao'pova.
S.PHON: te nʔs peeɗs esinʦs skʔs soʔpovs.
S.TRANSL: 還算是有稍微安撫的意思.

TRANSL.notes: (That money) was somewhat able to pacify (you).

S.TRANSL: was somewhat able to pacify .

S.FORM: maka ake'a ao'po'pova.
S.PHON: msks skeʔs soʔpoʔpovs.
S.FORM: maka ake'a ao'po'pova.
S.PHON: msks skeʔs soʔpoʔpovs.
S.TRANSL: 起碼稍微安撫一下.

TRANSL.notes: (The money/The Japanese government had to somewhat pacify (you) at least.

S.TRANSL: at least.

S.FORM: ci mio no kuzo.
S.PHON: ʦi mio no kuzo.
S.FORM: ci mio no kuzo.
S.PHON: ʦi mio no kuzo.
S.TRANSL: 這樣也沒什麼不好.
It's not so bad.

S.FORM: zou 'a mosu s'a no nia ohe niate auni no ang angumu.
S.PHON: zou ʔs mosu sʔs no nis ohe niste suni no sŋ sŋumu.
S.FORM: zou 'a mosu s'a no nia ohe niate auni no ang angumu.
S.PHON: zou ʔs mosu sʔs no nis ohe niste suni no sŋ sŋumu.
S.TRANSL: 你不是差一點被洋人幹掉啊?
Weren't you almost killed by western people?

S.FORM: anna'o uk'a ci mo 'so ahtu mokalu ho mo yu'susuyumo.
S.PHON: snnsʔo ukʔs ʦi mo ʔso shtu moksɗu ho mo juʔsusujumo.
S.FORM: anna'o uk'a ci mo 'so ahtu mokalu ho mo yu'susuyumo.
S.PHON: snnsʔo ukʔs ʦi mo ʔso shtu moksɗu ho mo juʔsusujumo.
S.TRANSL: 戰爭原本就沒有人真正佔到便宜.
In a war, by nature no one really takes advantage.

S.FORM: osu s'a eainca ak'i eno su nte cu c'o nte cu c'o nte cu c'o aʉmta auni no no amelika honcimu honci c'u te make.
S.PHON: osu sʔs esinʦs skʔi eno su nte ʦu ʦʔo nte ʦu ʦʔo nte ʦu ʦʔo sɨmts suni no no smeɗiks honʦimu honʦi ʦʔu te mske.
S.FORM: osu s'a eainca ak'i eno su nte cu c'o nte cu c'o nte cu c'o aʉmta auni no no amelika honcimu honci c'u te make.
S.PHON: osu sʔs esinʦs skʔi eno su nte ʦu ʦʔo nte ʦu ʦʔo nte ʦu ʦʔo sɨmts suni no no smeɗiks honʦimu honʦi ʦʔu te mske.

TRANSL.notes: Ak'i (外公)你不是有講過說,如果說你們失敗的話,你們幾乎要被美國人幹掉了?

S.TRANSL: Ak'i 你不是有講過說,如果說你們失敗的話,你們幾乎要被美國人幹掉了?
Grandfather, have you ever said that, if you had really failed, you would almost have been killed by the Americans?

S.FORM: ma o'a te cu c'o ah 'ana te s'a mako yikeyuei honcimza ako ma'nao isi cu atpʉta 'o maaya.
S.PHON: ms oʔs te ʦu ʦʔo sh ʔsns te sʔs msko jikejuei honʦimzs sko msʔnso isi ʦu stpɨts ʔo mssjs.
S.FORM: ma o'a te cu c'o ah 'ana te s'a mako yikeyuei honcimza ako ma'nao isi cu atpʉta 'o maaya.
S.PHON: ms oʔs te ʦu ʦʔo sh ʔsns te sʔs msko jikejuei honʦimzs sko msʔnso isi ʦu stpɨts ʔo mssjs.
S.TRANSL: 如果我們一直逗留在那邊,不到一個月日本人已經被打敗了.
If we had kept lingering there, Japanese would have been beaten within one month.

S.FORM: n'a eansoua oh.
S.PHON: nʔs esnsous oh.
S.FORM: n'a eansoua oh.
S.PHON: nʔs esnsous oh.
S.TRANSL: 我們還活著.
We are still alive.

S.FORM: ma asonʉ ancu ntoho teseupngʉ honci ntoho o'te cu atpʉta 'o maaya.
S.PHON: ms ssonɨ snʦu ntoho teseupŋɨ honʦi ntoho oʔte ʦu stpɨts ʔo mssjs.
S.FORM: ma asonʉ ancu ntoho teseupngʉ honci ntoho o'te cu atpʉta 'o maaya.
S.PHON: ms ssonɨ snʦu ntoho teseupŋɨ honʦi ntoho oʔte ʦu stpɨts ʔo mssjs.
S.TRANSL: 如果那個時候還沒有把日本人打敗的話,戰爭還沒有結束.
If the Japanese were not defeated at that time, the war would have not yet finished.

S.FORM: zou 'a isi cu no taipuya 'o maaya micu.

S.FORM: zou 'a isi cu no taipuya 'o maaya micu.

S.TRANSL: 對啊,那時候已經把日本人扳倒了.

TRANSL.notes: Yes, (at that time) Japanese had been knocked down.

S.TRANSL: Yes, Japanese had been knocked down.

S.FORM: pamaine'e?
S.PHON: psmsineʔe?

S.TRANSL: 讓你們回家嗎?

S.TRANSL: let you go home?

S.FORM: o'tena ehtothomʉ.

S.FORM: o'tena ehtothomʉ.

S.FORM: zou 'a momu s'a anana'o zua make?

S.FORM: WGS tiate atpʉtʉ.

S.FORM: WGS tiate atpʉtʉ.
S.PHON: **s tiste stpɨtɨ.

S.TRANSL: almost won.

S.FORM: ne apihana ohe eae taivang su su'uni ne nanyangshan ho ucia suyumi.

S.FORM: ne apihana ohe eae taivang su su'uni ne nanyangshan ho ucia suyumi.

S.TRANSL: 想要侵略從朝鮮到對岸,台灣,到南洋.

TRANSL.notes: (Japanese) wanted to invade (the areas) Korea, the other side of the Taiwan Strait, Taiwan and Nanyang.

S.TRANSL: wanted to invade Korea, the other side of the Taiwan Strait, Taiwan and Nanyang.

S.FORM: 'so a'ʉmta tau taunanavʉ na na nia cou nemio 'a.

S.TRANSL: 很欽佩當時的鄒人.

S.TRANSL: It's because was just digged. The mud went as knee's height.

TRANSL.notes: (Of all of us,) our shoes or slippers were all gone. We were all barefoot.

S.TRANSL: our shoes or slippers were all gone. We were all barefoot.

S.TRANSL: just made a short cut across bamboo forest.

S.TRANSL: ascended... because above there was a road.
